rationale: The citing paper explicitly studies horizon-dependent forecasting (e.g., 3/12/24 months) and reports that expense neglect is stronger at longer horizons, while the cited paper is a core study of nonlinear subjective time perception across objective horizons and its effects on intertemporal judgments. With only a DOI-level match and no local citation context, this could be a meaningful mechanism/theory citation rather than an obvious incidental mention, but the current evidence is not enough to confidently classify as clearly substantive.
